name: Writing (Early Systems)
tagline: Visible marks that store language across time — from tokens to cuneiform and hieroglyphs
problem:
Speech fades when the breath stops. Accounting for granaries, tithes, and loans needed tallies that survived seasons. Early writing solves external memory: marks on clay, stone, or papyrus that rehydrate meaning in competent readers.

The obstacle shifts from air to material: durable substrates, standardised signs, and scribal training. Pictographic and syllabic systems trade learnability for compactness; mastering thousands of signs is a profession.

Writing also enables law codes and propaganda — power concentrates around literate castes until literacy spreads.
overview:
Proto-writing used clay tokens then impressed tablets; cuneiform pressed wedge strokes into wet clay; Egyptian hieroglyphs mixed logographic and phonetic signs. Rebus principle maps sound of pictured word to unrelated homonyms — bridge to syllabaries.

Scribes used styluses, palettes, and firing kilns for tablets. Copying errors become manuscript traditions; commentaries fossilise readings.

Literacy rates stayed tiny for millennia; still, writing let empires administer distance — orders could outlive couriers if sealed and authenticated.

Decipherment today uses bilinguals like Rosetta parallels — a warning to archive dictionaries, not just texts.

principles.name: Rebus and phonetisation
principles.explanation: Pictures of things whose names sound like abstract words bootstrap syllabic spelling.

principles.name: Determinatives
principles.explanation: Silent classifiers disambiguate homographic logograms — early error correction.

principles.name: Material constraints
principles.explanation: Clay encourages wedge strokes; papyrus encourages ink flows — script shape is partly substrate physics.

principles.name: Orthographic depth
principles.explanation: Mapping from graphemes to phonemes can be opaque — reading becomes partially memorised lexicon even in alphabets.

rawMaterials.name: Clay and temper
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Sintered regolith tablets if humour permits
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Levigate; wedge while leather-hard; fire optional

buildSteps.title: Standardise sign list
buildSteps.description: Choose finite repertory; document stroke order; forbid idiosyncratic squiggles in official tablets.

buildSteps.title: Prepare tablets
buildSteps.description: Uniform thickness; smooth face; control drying rate to avoid curling.
buildSteps.warningNote: Air bubbles pop during firing — inspect leather-hard

buildSteps.title: Teach copying cycles
buildSteps.description: Dictation then independent composition; peer marking reduces drift.
buildSteps.warningNote: Dyslexia analogues exist — rotate teaching methods

buildSteps.title: Archive with indexes
buildSteps.description: Edge marks, tags, and shelved orientation speed retrieval.
buildSteps.warningNote: Flood and fire are symmetric threats — duplicate sites

buildSteps.title: Publish law and ritual corpora
buildSteps.description: Visible stelae synchronise public meaning; reduces elite-only drift.
buildSteps.warningNote: Propaganda risk — pair with critique institutions

history.event: Early hieroglyphic inscription on labels in Abydos contexts.

impact: Writing turned speech into inventory — law, recipe, and insult alike. It also created scribal power until alphabets and printing widened access.

videos.title: Cuneiform and early writing
videos.description: Museum educator videos on clay tablet production.
